growth_records.strain: B31 A3 (WT; lacks cp9; Adams lab strain PA003) carrying empty vector p (WT/p)
growth_records.medium: Barbour-Stoenner-Kelly II (BSK-II) medium supplemented with gelatin and 6% rabbit serum
growth_records.cite: Zamba-Campero et al. Broadly conserved FlgV controls flagellar assembly and Borrelia burgdorferi dissemination in mice. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-54806-w. [PMC11607428]
growth_records.snippet: a significant, prolonged doubling time for ΔflgV spirochetes in lag phase (8.5 h), compared to WT spirochetes in lag phase (4.6 h)
growth_records.notes: WT lag-phase doubling time (4.6 h) explicitly reported (dark-field microscopy enumeration growth curve, Fig. S4F). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/4.6 = 0.151 h-1. Note this is a lag-phase doubling time, not exponential-phase mu; culture conditions (BSKII, 35C, 2.5% CO2, microaerophilic) taken from Methods 'Bacterial strains and growth conditions' section of same paper.

growth_records.strain: B31 A3 ΔflgV/p (flgV deletion mutant carrying empty vector)
growth_records.medium: Barbour-Stoenner-Kelly II (BSK-II) medium supplemented with gelatin and 6% rabbit serum
growth_records.cite: Zamba-Campero et al. Broadly conserved FlgV controls flagellar assembly and Borrelia burgdorferi dissemination in mice. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-54806-w. [PMC11607428]
growth_records.snippet: a significant, prolonged doubling time for ΔflgV spirochetes in lag phase (8.5 h), compared to WT spirochetes in lag phase (4.6 h)
growth_records.notes: ΔflgV lag-phase doubling time (8.5 h) explicitly reported (dark-field microscopy enumeration growth curve, Fig. S4F). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/8.5 = 0.082 h-1. Note this is a lag-phase doubling time, not exponential-phase mu; culture conditions (BSKII, 35C, 2.5% CO2, microaerophilic) taken from Methods section of same paper.
